column5: description

column1: # Lines starting with # are ignored. Blank lines are ignored.

column5: Rincewind, the Disc's worst wizard, is press-ganged into guiding the naively optimistic tourist Twoflower across a world balanced on four elephants atop a giant turtle.
A deadly red star approaches the Disc and only a single spell locked inside Rincewind's head can save it, if he can survive long enough to use it.
A dying wizard accidentally bequeaths his staff and powers to a girl, who must fight the entrenched prejudice of Unseen University to be admitted as a wizard.
Death takes on an apprentice named Mort, who makes the critical mistake of falling in love and deciding to override fate by refusing to let someone die.
The eighth son of an eighth son of a wizard is a sourcerer of terrifying raw power, threatening to return the Disc to the dangerous age of magic.
Three witches in the kingdom of Lancre become entangled in a royal succession crisis, a murdered king, a stolen crown, and a theatre troupe with an inconvenient play.
A young Assassin's Guild graduate unexpectedly inherits the ancient kingdom of Djelibeybi and must contend with its all-consuming obsession with pyramids and the dead.
The threadbare Night Watch of Ankh-Morpork faces a full-grown dragon summoned by a secret brotherhood, led by a new recruit who believes in truth and justice.
The Disc's only demonologist attempts to summon a demon to grant three wishes and instead gets Rincewind, resulting in chaos across time, space, and the Dungeon Dimensions.
Holy Wood hill begins producing moving pictures with an eerie compulsion that draws people from across the Disc into a silver-screen fantasy with a dark purpose.
Death is issued a retirement notice and must live as a mortal farm hand, while the Disc, denied death, begins to suffer from an accumulation of unspent life force.
Granny Weatherwax and the Lancre witches travel to the distant city of Genua to prevent a fairy godmother from forcing a happy ending on a young woman who doesn't want one.
The great god Om finds himself trapped in the body of a small tortoise with only one true believer left — a simple novice named Brutha — as the Omnian Inquisition wages holy war.
The elves — beautiful, cold, and utterly without mercy — attempt to force their way back into the Disc through a gap in reality near Lancre, and only the witches stand in their way.
The Night Watch investigates a series of murders connected to a new and terrible weapon called a gonne, as Ankh-Morpork's new diversity initiative complicates policing.
Music with rocks in arrives in Ankh-Morpork with a life of its own, and Death's adopted granddaughter Susan must understand what it truly means for something to die.
Rincewind is magically transported to the Agatean Empire, where revolution is stirring and a prophecy about the Great Wizzard is about to cause enormous diplomatic problems.
Agnes Nitt joins the Ankh-Morpork Opera House and discovers it is haunted, while Granny Weatherwax and Nanny Ogg investigate from Lancre with a cookbook as cover.
Commander Vimes investigates a series of murders and a plot to poison Lord Vetinari, uncovering questions about free will and identity among the city's golem population.
The Hogfather — the Disc's equivalent of Father Christmas — has gone missing, and Death must take his place while his granddaughter Susan hunts down what stole him.
Ankh-Morpork stumbles toward a pointless war over a tiny island, and Commander Vimes must navigate politics, patriotism, and a device that lets him see two futures at once.
Rincewind is stranded on XXXX, a continent at the end of the world running on narrative clichés, while the Unseen University wizards search for him somewhere in the distant past.
A family of progressive, self-help vampires invades the kingdom of Lancre, and Granny Weatherwax — whose faith is wavering — may be the only one who can resist their influence.
Vimes travels to Uberwald as Ankh-Morpork's reluctant ambassador and uncovers a conspiracy involving the dwarf clans, a missing replica, and the werewolf political situation.
William de Worde accidentally invents the newspaper in Ankh-Morpork and finds himself investigating a conspiracy to frame Lord Vetinari for murder.
A master clockmaker is commissioned to build the first truly accurate clock, which would stop time itself — and only the History Monks and a young apprentice can prevent it.
Cohen the Barbarian and his Silver Horde plan to return fire to the gods atop Cori Celesti, which would destroy the Disc, forcing an unlikely rescue mission in a primitive spacecraft.

column1: The Amazing Maurice and His Educated Rodents
column5: A streetwise cat leads a group of self-aware rats and an earnest pied-piper boy in a profitable scam that goes dangerously wrong in a town where something has already eaten the rats.
Commander Vimes is thrown back in time to his younger self's era and must navigate the Glorious Revolution of the 25th of May while keeping his own history on track.
Nine-year-old Tiffany Aching armed with a frying pan and aided by a clan of tiny blue pictsies must rescue her baby brother from the Queen of the Fairies.
A girl disguises herself as a boy to enlist and find her missing brother in a beleaguered army fighting a war for a country run entirely on religious zealotry.
Tiffany Aching leaves home to apprentice with a real witch and is possessed by a hiver, a bodiless creature that has been hunting minds for millennia.
Moist von Lipwig, a career conman, is given a choice — death or taking charge of Ankh-Morpork's long-derelict Post Office and making it work.
Vimes investigates the murder of a dwarf deep in the mines as centuries-old tensions between dwarfs and trolls threaten to reignite into full-scale street war.
Tiffany Aching steps into a dance meant only for the seasons and the Wintersmith — the spirit of winter — falls in destructive obsessive love with her.
Moist von Lipwig is press-ganged into running Ankh-Morpork's Royal Bank and its broken mint, with a small dog as majority shareholder and the Lavish family plotting against him.
The wizards of Unseen University must learn to play foot-the-ball or lose their ancient right to a free dinner, while a young man from the Night Kitchen changes everything.
Tiffany Aching faces the Cunning Man, an ancient malevolent force that turns communities against witches, while dealing with the very human complications of being the local witch.
Commander Vimes takes a forced holiday in the countryside and finds himself investigating the trafficking and murder of goblins, a people nobody considers worth protecting.
The steam engine comes to the Disc, and Moist von Lipwig must manage its rapid proliferation while a dwarfish fundamentalist movement violently resists all change.
Tiffany Aching faces her greatest challenge as the elves, emboldened by loss in the world of humans, attempt to reclaim the Disc and the witches must close ranks.

column1: # A Song of Ice and Fire — George R.R. Martin

column5: Noble houses across Westeros war for the Iron Throne while in the frozen north an ancient evil stirs, and a exiled princess plots her return across the Narrow Sea.
The realm fractures as five kings press their claims simultaneously, a comet blazes across the sky, and dragons are reborn for the first time in over a century.
The war of five kings reaches its bloody climax through treachery, impossible battles, and a series of deaths that shatter every expectation of how such stories end.
The aftermath of war leaves the Seven Kingdoms economically ruined and politically unstable as new and dangerous players make their moves on a weakened board.
Daenerys struggles to rule the slave city of Meereen as her dragons grow uncontrollable, while Jon Snow faces threats from beyond the Wall and from within the Night's Watch.
Field Marshal Tamas leads a bloody coup against the corrupt Adran monarchy, only to discover the gods of the Dynize people may be real and are returning to reclaim the world.
Tamas and his powder mages are cut off behind enemy lines while his son Taniel fights addiction and conspiracy in the capital as the Adran war effort begins to collapse.
The gods walk the earth, foreign armies occupy the capital, and Tamas must unite powder mages, privileged sorcerers, and common soldiers for a final desperate defence.
A new generation of soldiers and powder mages uncover a conspiracy deep in the occupied Dynize continent that could plunge the world back into divine war.
The Dynize invade Landfall and an unlikely alliance of powder mages, bone-eyes, and street criminals must hold the city against an enemy that controls the power of gods.
Michel Bravis works as a spy inside the Dynize occupation while Ben Styke and the Mad Lancers fight a brutal guerrilla war to drive the conquerors from Landfall.

column1: # General fiction — diverse sample for cross-genre similarity

column5: A totalitarian society surveils and controls every aspect of human life through propaganda, doublethink, and the systematic destruction of truth and memory.
A future society engineered for frictionless stability through genetic conditioning, pleasure drugs, and the elimination of family, art, and individual aspiration.
A theocratic regime strips women of all rights and identities; the tale of one handmaid's resistance narrated under conditions where even writing is forbidden.
A desert planet holds the universe's most precious resource; the fate of empire, ecology, religion, and humanity converge on a boy who may be the prophesied messiah.
A mathematician predicts the inevitable fall of galactic civilisation and plants twin seeds of recovery to shorten the dark age from thirty thousand years to one thousand.
A legendary wizard recounts his own story — from orphaned street child to the most famous man alive — in a world where knowing the true name of a thing gives power over it.
A small-town Alabama lawyer defends a Black man falsely accused of rape in the 1930s, witnessed through the eyes of his young daughter Scout growing into moral understanding.
Seven generations of the Buendia family found, inhabit, and are undone by the mythical town of Macondo in a story where the miraculous and the mundane are indistinguishable.
A father and son walk south through the ash-covered ruins of America, carrying the fire of human decency against a world that has almost entirely extinguished it.
A sweeping history of the human species from the cognitive revolution to the present, questioning whether the pursuit of progress has made us any happier.
